About The Program

Taiwan ICDF International Higher Education Scholarship Program 2026 is an opportunity offered by the International Cooperation and Development Fund (TaiwanICDF). It provides scholarships to outstanding students for higher education in cooperation with renowned partner universities in Taiwan. The selected candidates will receive a monthly allowance of up to TWD 20,000 and other benefits.

Source: icdf.org.tw

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 15-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be a citizen of India
secure their highest-level diploma certificate before 31st July 2026
fulfil the requirements of the partner university where they have applied to study with the scholarship
fulfil the resident visa requirements set by the Bureau of Consular Affairs, Ministry of Foreign Affairs
fulfil the requirements for an Alien Resident Certificate (ARC) set by the Ministry of the Interior, of the ROC (Taiwan) government

Note:-

Students who previously received a MOFA Taiwan Scholarship, a MOE scholarship, or a TaiwanICDF scholarship must have graduated by 31st July 2025 to be eligible for this scholarship in 2026.
Students applying for this scholarship in a row are ineligible in 2026.
Students who previously had their scholarship revoked by any ROC (Taiwan) government agency or related institute or expelled from a university are ineligible.
Students holding another ROC (Taiwan) government-sponsored scholarship during the TaiwanICDF scholarship are ineligible.
Benefits

The selected candidates will receive:-

Economy class airfare to and fro Taiwan
Residence in student dormitories
Full tuition fee waiver
Credit fee waiver
Insurance fee for the entire duration of a student’s program
The costs of textbooks purchased, subject to the approval of the director of the institute
A fixed monthly allowance to the student's local bank account for food and living costs in the following way: 
Bacherlor's Program: NT$15,000
Master's Program: NT$18,000
Ph.D. Program: NT$20,000
Documents
Passport or other document showing proof of nationality
Highest-level diploma and academic transcripts
English language proficiency certificate
Referee's information
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can follow these steps to apply:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID)
Step 3: Click 'TaiwanICDF Scholarship Online Application System'.
Step 4: Follow the specified steps to submit the application.

Note:-

Applicants must submit a separate application to their chosen university before the respective university deadline.
Detailed information about the university applications is available in the 'University Details' document below.
Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Start: 1 December 2025
Application Deadline: 15 March 2026
Informing of Final Candidates by: 10 June 2025

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected by the following process:

Application review by the ROC (Taiwan) Embassy/Office
Recommendation by the Joint Recruitment Committee
Informing and delivering acceptance letters to final candidates by TaiwanICDF

Selected candidates must review and sign the consent form, then submit it to the TaiwanICDF via the R.O.C. Embassy/Representative Office in their country. Signing indicates acceptance of the TaiwanICDF Scholarship terms and confirms official scholarship status.

About The Program

The Contemporary Taiwan Fellowship at Stanford University’s Shorenstein Asia-Pacific Research Center (APARC) offers outstanding scholars the opportunity to conduct advanced research on contemporary Taiwan. This fellowship supports individuals whose academic work focuses on Taiwan’s political, economic, or social developments, as well as its international relations and cross-strait dynamics. The selected fellows will receive the award of up to USD 30,000 (approx. ₹26,62,923) along with other benefits.

Source: aparc.fsi.stanford.edu

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Mar-2026

To be eligible, applicants must:-

be academics, researchers, or professionals working on contemporary Taiwan-related research
hold a Ph.D. degree or equivalent professional experience
have a strong research record in relevant fields such as political science, economics, sociology, or international studies
be proficient in English 

 

Note:- Preference is given to scholars whose research contributes to understanding Taiwan’s domestic and global developments.

Benefits

The selected fellows will receive the award of up to USD 30,000 (approx. ₹26,62,923) along with other benefits.

Documents
Passport
Completed application form
Detailed research proposal
Curriculum Vitae (CV)
Writing sample 
Two letters of recommendation
How can you apply?

Eligible candidates can apply by following these steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Scroll down to the Apply for the Visiting Fellowship and click on the ‘APPLICATION FORM’.
Step 4: Click on ‘Begin’ and complete the application form.
Step 5: Fill in the required information and attach the documents, and email them to the ‘taiwanprogramfellowship@stanford.edu’.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 1 March 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on:-

Relevance and originality of proposed research
Academic merit and previous achievements
Contribution to the understanding of contemporary Taiwan
Alignment with APARC’s mission and faculty expertise

About The Program

The National University of Singapore Amgen Scholars Program 2026 is an opportunity offered by Amgen Foundation and Harvard University to students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate courses. This scholarship aims to help students complete their education at the university. The selected candidates shall receive a stipend of SGD 6000 and other benefits.

Source: amgen.com

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be enrolled as an undergraduate (or equivalent) at a university in an eligible Asian country or area
return to home institution for at least one semester to continue undergraduate studies after completing the program
have completed at least one year of undergraduate study by June 2, 2026.
be available to attend full-time from June 2 to August 6, 2026, with no other engagements during this period.
have good working knowledge of English, demonstrated by either:
All undergraduate courses taught in English, or
Acceptable scores in one of the following tests:
TOEFL iBT: 72 or above
IELTS: 5.5 overall band score or above
Cambridge English FCE
TOEIC: 1095 or above
TOEIC L&R: 785 or above
TOEIC S&W: 310 or above
Benefits

The selected candidates shall receive:

Accommodation arranged by the National University of Singapore.
Travel Grant up to SGD 1,500 to cover airfare from the participant’s home country to Singapore and from Japan back to the home country after the symposium, via the most direct and economical route.
Stipend of SGD 6,000 (₹4,20,737 approx.) for the 8‑week program.
Visa Application: The National University of Singapore will initiate the TEP application process and cover all associated fees.

About The Program

The Oskar Morgenstern Fellowship 2026 is offered by the Mercatus Center at George Mason University. The fellowship is a prestigious one-year online program designed to support outstanding graduate students, scholars, and practitioners from any discipline with an interest in political economy and emerging technologies, such as artificial intelligence. Selected fellows will receive a stipend of up to $2,250 along with all required readings for the program.

Source: mercatus.org

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 15-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must be:

a graduate student in any discipline and from any university pursuing research on emerging technologies and AI, or
an active scholar at an academic or research organisation who teaches or does research on emerging technologies and AI, or
an active practitioner in research or policy related to emerging technologies and AI, or
be enrolled in an accredited PhD program during the upcoming academic year

Note: Fellows must have training in quantitative methods.

Benefits

The selected fellows will receive a stipend of up to $2,250 (approx. ₹2,01,697.88, along with all required readings for the program.

Documents
Cover letter (1-2 pages)
Curriculum Vitae
Short answer questions responses

About The Program

Radboud Scholarship Programme 2026-27 is an opportunity offered by Radboud University for students who are willing to pursue an English-taught Master's programme at the university. The selected students will receive reduced tuition fees and additional benefits.

Source: ru.nl

Eligibility

Note:- Applicants must not have obtained a degree from any university in the Netherlands or have not received any previous education in the Netherlands (exchange programmes excluded, provided that they are part of the Bachelor's degree achieved outside the Netherlands).

Note:-

The costs of living and studying in the Netherlands are not covered by the scholarship.
18 scholarships are available for 2026-27.

About The Program

The Scholarship Programme for Diaspora Children (SPDC) 2025-26 is offered by the Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India. This prestigious scholarship supports outstanding students of the Indian diaspora who want to pursue undergraduate courses in Indian universities and institutes. Selected students will receive up to $4,000 per year towards tuition fees, admission fees, and post-admission charges.

Source: mea.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 30-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be a child of Persons of Indian Origin (PIO)/Overseas Citizens of India (OCI)/Non-Resident Indians (NRI) OR a child of Indian workers in ECR countries (Afghanistan, Bahrain, Indonesia, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Libya, Malaysia, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Sudan, Syria, Thailand, United Arab Emirates, and Yemen)
be in the age group of 17 to 21 years as on 31 July 2025
have passed grades 11th and 12th from abroad (except children of Indian workers in ECR countries who studied in India may have completed their education in India from an AIU-recognised institution)
have secured a minimum of 60% aggregate marks or equivalent grades in the Senior Secondary (10+2) examination
be enrolled in a full-time undergraduate degree program in designated courses
have secured admission in Central Universities of India, 'A' Grade NAAC-accredited institutions recognised by UGC, or NITs/SPAs/IIITs through the DASA Scheme
have total household monthly income should not exceed $5,000 (for NRIs/PIOs/OCIs) or $3,000 (for children of Indian workers in ECR countries)
not be receiving any other scholarship or financial assistance under any State Government or Central Government of India scheme

Note:-

Wards of Government of India employees, Financial Institutions, Banks, and PSUs working abroad are not eligible.
Only regular offline undergraduate courses are eligible.
For Medicine and related courses, applications can be submitted in the 2nd year of study.
Children of Indians residing in Nepal shall be eligible for SPDC on fulfilment of the following two criteria:
Both parents should be registered in the Indian Mission/Post in Nepal for a minimum period of two years.
The applicant has completed at least two years of schooling in classes 11th & 12th in Nepal before applying.
Criteria fixed for the NRI sub-category will apply to the Children of Indians residing in Nepal.
Only regular offline undergraduate courses are eligible for assistance/scholarship under SPDC.
The ward of any employee of Govt. of India, Financial Institutions, Banks, PSUs and any other body of the Govt. of India living/working abroad will not be covered under SPDC.
Benefits

The selected students will receive financial assistance to the extent of 75% of the total Institutional Economic Cost (IEC), subject to a maximum of $4,000 (approx. ₹3,60,737.80) per annum. The IEC includes tuition fee, hostel fee, and other institutional charges (food charges excluded). The scholarship is awarded for the entire duration of undergraduate courses, with continuation in subsequent years based on academic performance.


Continuation criteria for 2nd year onwards:

Students securing 50% and above aggregate marks: Receive maximum admissible scholarship (75% of IEC or $4,000, whichever is less)
Students securing less than 50% but with clear pass marks: Receive 90% of the maximum admissible scholarship
Students promoted without clear pass marks in all subjects: Receive 50% of the maximum admissible scholarship
Students not promoted to the next academic year: Not eligible for further scholarship
Documents
Academic transcript
Passing certificates of 11th and 12th standard examinations
Valid passport copy
Parents work permit
For PIOs/OCIs: Copy of PIO/OCI card or declaration about proof of Indian origin attested by the Head of Indian Mission
For NRIs: Copy of NRI certificate
For children of Indian workers in ECR countries: Copy of valid work permit of parent(s) and proof of stay for the last two years
Self-attested annual salary certificate of parents signed by the employer (or Income Tax Return for self-employed)
Declaration that the applicant is not receiving any other scholarship (Appendix C)
Passport photograph (200 x 230 pixels preferred)
Admission confirmation letter from the Indian institution
Institutional Undertaking ( on institutional letterhead)
Declaration Form  by Parent/ Guardian

Note: For further details, please refer to the ‘Guidelines’ document.

How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Scroll down to ‘SPDC Application Form’ and click it to download.
Step 4: Complete the application form in English along with all required documents.
Step 5: Submit the completed application form with all supporting documents to the respective Indian Embassy/High Commission/Consulate.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 30th January 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on merit-cum-means basis. When two applicants secure equal marks, preference will be given to the applicant from a lower-income family. The scholarship has category-wise and gender-wise reservations, with 50% slots earmarked for female students.

Terms and Conditions
Only one application per candidate is allowed, and incomplete or ineligible applications will be rejected.
The eligibility of candidates in doubtful cases will be decided by the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA). 
Employees of the Government of India, Financial Institutions, Banks, Public Sector Undertakings, and other government bodies living or working abroad cannot apply for the SPDC.
Candidates receiving any scholarship or financial assistance from the State or Central Government of India are not eligible for assistance under the SPDC.
Selected Persons of Indian Origin (excluding Overseas Citizens of India) must obtain a valid student visa from Indian Missions or Posts abroad by presenting the admission offer letter from their respective institutes.
Students currently receiving scholarships will continue to do so according to existing guidelines until they complete their courses, provided they remain eligible.
The medium of instruction at all institutes is English.
Shortlisted applicants must consent to acceptance within 7 days of the publication of the selection list, and within 15 days, they must provide an undertaking from their institution.
If false information is discovered, the scholarship will be revoked, and any disbursed funds will be recovered.
The MEA's decision on shortlisting is final and binding.

FAQ's

Question: What does PIO/OCI mean?
Answer: Person of Indian Origin (PIO)/Overseas Citizen of India refers to citizens of countries other than India (excluding Pakistan and Bangladesh) who have ever held an Indian passport. This includes those whose parents or grandparents were citizens of India, as defined by the Constitution of India or Section 2(B) of the Citizenship Act, 1955 (Act No. 57 of 1955).

Question: What does NRI mean?
Answer: Non-Resident Indian (NRI) refers to Indian citizens who have been outside India for the past 9 years or 730 days during the last 7 years.

Question: Is the scholarship renewable?

About The Program

The DRDO DIAT Post-doctoral Fellowship 2026 is an opportunity provided by the Defence Institute of Advanced Technology (DIAT) to students holding a PhD degree. The selected students will receive A fellowship of up to ₹55,000 per month and other benefits  
 
Source: diat.ac.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 27-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

hold a Ph.D. degree awarded within the last three years, or have submitted a Ph.D. thesis (applicants about to submit are eligible, but selection is subject to thesis submission before the award offer's validity expires)
have research publications in high-impact-factor SCI journals
be an Indian national, Person of Indian Origin (PIO), or Overseas Citizen of India (OCI); foreign nationals may be considered for up to 20% of fellowships
not exceed 35 years of age as of the application deadline (relaxable by up to 5 years for SC/ST/Physically disabled/women candidates and 3 years for OBC Non-Creamy Layer candidates)
Benefits

The selected candidates will receive:

A monthly fellowship of Rs. 55,000 + HRA (or Rs. 35,000 + HRA for those who have submitted their thesis but not yet received the degree), tenable initially for 2 years as a purely temporary assignment
An annual research grant of Rs. 2,00,000 (for minor equipment, consumables, contingencies, and domestic travel; no manpower support provided)
Annual overheads of Rs. 1,00,000
(Note: Fellows must undertake research objectives independently and seek DIAT consent for absences exceeding eight continuous weeks, except for project-related fieldwork.)

Documents
Duly filled application form
Curriculum Vitae (CV) with complete list of publications
PDF files of 2-5 important publications
Proposed research plan (up to 500 words)
PDF of Ph.D. degree certificate, provisional Ph.D. certificate, or thesis submission certificate
Duly filled application (additional copy if specified)
How can you apply?

Eligible candidates can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Download the application form from the Institute’s website at 
http://www.diat.ac.in
Step 4: Complete the form in the prescribed proforma and forward it in a sealed envelope, superscribed “Application for Admission to Post Doctoral Programme in the Department / School of ______,” to the Joint Registrar (Academics), Defence Institute of Advanced Technology, Girinagar, Pune 411025. 
(Note:- Completed applications must reach DIAT on or before January 27, 2026.)

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: January 27, 2026

Important documents
Application
Terms and Conditions
Candidates who have obtained their Ph.D. from DIAT are not eligible for a Post-Doctoral Fellowship (PDF) at DIAT under this scheme.
Selected candidates must join within two months of the award's effective date.
The Post-Doctoral Fellowship requires full-time research work of a standard significantly higher than Ph.D.-level work.
Post-Doctoral Fellows will undergo annual progress evaluations.
All publications, patents, or reports resulting from research at DIAT must acknowledge DIAT's support.
Post-Doctoral Fellows must assist the department, including supporting Ph.D. and postgraduate students, and providing laboratory and conference assistance.

About The Program

The Stanford Next Asia Policy Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026-28 is offered by the Stanford Next Asia Policy Lab (SNAPL) at the Shorenstein Asia-Pacific Research Centre (APARC), Stanford University. The fellowship supports emerging scholars engaged in interdisciplinary, problem-oriented, and policy-relevant research on Asia’s contemporary challenges. Selected fellows will receive USD 80,000 per year plus benefits and access to Stanford research facilities for a two-year appointment.

Source: shorenstein.aparc.stanford.edu

Eligibility
Deadline Date: Closed
To be eligible, an applicant must:-
have completed a Ph.D. degree within three years of the fellowship,
hold a Ph.D. in social sciences, humanities, or related disciplines, with demonstrated interest in Asian policy research
show strong academic merit, research productivity, and interest in interdisciplinary collaboration
have their dissertation submitted and approved for conferral by June 30, 2026, and certification of degree completion submitted no later than August 31, 2026


Note:- Applicants may apply for one or more of the following fellowship tracks:

Talent Flows and Development
Nationalism and Racism
U.S.-Asia Relations
Democratic Crisis and Reform
Benefits

Selected fellows will receive a USD 80,000 annual salary and other benefits.

Documents
Passport
Formal letter of request (research statement)
Full academic CV
Three letters of recommendation

Benefits

A stipend of ₹25,344 per month.

Selection Criteria

The fellows are selected based on the following criteria:-

Believer in equitable and excellent education
Demonstrated leadership and openness to learning
Strong organisational and collaboration skills
Courageous with problem-solving abilities
Boldness and ambition

Furthermore, the selection process is divided into three stages:-

Stage 1: Application Form - Applicants must introduce themselves by providing information about their academic background, work experiences, reasons for wanting to join the fellowship, and their interests.

Stage 2: Telephonic Interview - After the online test, the selected applicants will be called for a 30-minute telephonic interview.

Stage 3: Final Assessment - In the final stage, the selected candidates will participate in various activities with Teach For India and other applicants. These activities include sample teaching, critical thinking exercises, group activities, and interviews.

Terms and Conditions
The fellowship is a paid, full-time program for 2 years.
Prior teaching experience is not mandatory for the fellowship. The selected candidates will be provided with the necessary training.
The selected candidates will be placed in one of the 8 cities (Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Chennai, Delhi, Gurgaon, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai or Pune) for the duration of the fellowship.
Fellows are not allowed to engage in external professional or academic activities for the entire duration of the fellowship.
The fellows will be provided with multiple opportunities upon completion of their fellowship.
Applicants shall hear back about the outcome of their application via email within 15 working days from the submission date.

About The Program

Post Matric Scholarship to OBC Students, Maharashtra 2025-26 is an opportunity offered by the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra to financially help Other Backward Classes (OBC) students studying in Class 11 and above. The scheme aims to foster a passion for higher education among the students by providing them with financial assistance for their educational expenses. The selected students will receive a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹425 and other benefits.

Source: mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be an Indian citizen
be a domicile of Maharashtra
belong to the Other Backward Classes (OBC) category
be studying in Class 11 and higher in courses approved by the government
have secured 75% attendance for the current year
have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹1,50,000
Benefits

Eligible students will be paid maintenance allowance as follows:-

Course	Hostellers (in ₹, per month for 10 months)	Day Scholars (in ₹, per month for 10 months)
Group A	425	190
Group B	290	190
Group C	290	190
Group D	230	120
Group E	150	90

Note:-

The scholarship amount will be given to the students from the admission date to till exam completion date.
50% of tuition fees and exam fees will be paid to OBC applicants who have been admitted to government-approved non-granted colleges in professional courses.
The students will receive 100% of the tuition fee, exam fee, and maintenance allowance to study in government/government-aided institutions in professional and non-professional courses.
100% benefit (tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance) is applicable for D.Ed. and B.Ed. courses. For students studying in government-aided /non-aided D.Ed. and B.Ed courses, the fee structure is applicable as per government rates for the same course. 
If the applicant takes an admission on or before the 20th of any month, the maintenance allowance will be issued for that current month. Otherwise, maintenance allowance will be entitled from next month.
Maintenance allowance and exam fees are disbursed to the applicant upon promotion to the next higher class.
Documents
Caste certificate issued by a competent authority
Income certificate/declaration issued by a competent authority
Caste validity certificate
Marksheet of HSC or SSC or last examination
Gap certificate (Not mandatory, but required in case of a gap)
Father/Guardian's death certificate (if applicable)
Ration card
Leaving certificate
Declaration certificate of parents/guardians about the number of children beneficiaries

Note:- 

Caste validity certificate is mandatory for professional degree courses and professional postgraduate courses. However, it is not mandatory for non-professional courses.
A maximum of 2 male children from a family are allowed to avail of this scholarship. For female children, this rule does not apply.
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the 'New Applicant Registration' button at the right-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 4: Fill in all required details, verify the email and the phone number, enter the captcha and click on the  'Register' button.
Step 5: After successfully registering on the portal, click on the 'Applicant login' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard.
Step 6: Log in using the username and password. 
Step 7: Fill in the necessary details, upload the required documents, and submit.

Apply Now

Application Deadline: 31 March 2026

Selection Criteria

The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria.

Important documents
Scholarship Guidelines (In Marathi)
Terms and Conditions
The applicants are required to pass the previous year's examination for the renewal of the scholarship.
If an applicant fails for a year, no reimbursement is provided for that specific year.
If the applicants get admission to a government hostel, they will be eligible for one-third amount of hostlers.
Applicants should come through the CAP round for professional courses.
Scholarships/freeship will continue until the applicant completes one course.
If a student fails a particular academic year, they will still receive the tuition fees, exam fees, and maintenance allowance for that year. However, they will not receive any further financial assistance until they have successfully moved on to the next grade level.
No scholarship will be paid to the applicants under this scheme who are already receiving a scholarship/stipend from another source.
The applicants who change from professional to non-professional courses will not receive any scholarship.
If an applicant changes their course in between academic years, they will not be eligible for further assistance.
If a student is unable to take their annual exams due to a medical condition or an unforeseen event, the college principal may grant them a pass on the condition that they provide satisfactory medical documentation or other sufficient evidence to support their claim.
Scholarships or freeships will continue to be provided to students until they complete one course, such as 11th or 12th grade Arts, B.A., M.A., M.Phil., or Ph.D. If a student completes both B.A. and B.Ed. degrees and later decide to pursue an M.A., they will no longer be eligible for scholarship/freeship benefits. However, if a student opts for an M.B.A. following their B.Ed., they may be eligible for scholarship or freeship support, as it is considered a professional postgraduate course.
For Group A, if an applicant pursuing a Group A course fails in the examination for the first time, the award may be renewed. However, for second and subsequent failures in any class, the applicant shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class.
For Group B, C, D, and E, the applicant must secure a promotion to a higher class. In case of second and subsequent failures in any class, the applicant shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class.

About The Program

The CAS-ANSO Scholarship (Degree Program) 2026 is jointly offered by the Alliance of International Science Organisations (ANSO) and the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS). It supports talented international students pursuing Master’s or Doctoral degrees at the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC), the University of Chinese Academy of Sciences (UCAS), or other CAS institutes. The scholarship aims to cultivate young scientists and promote global collaboration in science and technology. Selected students will receive a monthly stipend of up to CNY 7000 a month, depending on their respective courses, along with other benefits.

Source: anso.org.cn

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be an international citizen (not hold Chinese citizenship)
be proficient in English or Chinese
be born on or after 1 January 1996 (Master’s); on or after 1 January 1991 (Ph.D.)
meet the admission criteria of USTC or UCAS for international students
not hold another degree in progress at any university in China


Note:- Applicants cannot apply to both USTC and UCAS simultaneously.

Benefits

Selected students will receive:-

Tuition waiver
Monthly stipend as follows:    
Ph.D. Program -CNY 6000 (approx.₹74,764.20) to CNY 7000 (approx ₹87,224.90) per month, up to 48 months.
Postgraduate - 3000 CNY (approx.₹37382.10) per month, up to 36 months.
Health insurance
Application fee waiver
International travel allowance/reimbursement from home country to China (one-time)
One-time VISA/residence allowance permit
Documents
Completed application form (via USTC/UCAS portal)
Passport copy
Academic transcripts and certificates
Curriculum Vitae (CV)
Two letters of recommendation
Research proposal
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Application Procedure' (Part IV) and click on the USTC or UCAS admission system link (either graduate or postgraduate applicants).
Step 4: Create your account in the online application system.
Step 5: Click on 'Create an Account' and enter the required details to create your account. 
Step 6: Enter the email ID and password to Login.
Step 7: Fill in the required details and attach the supporting documents along with the online application form.
Note: Students can apply for the scholarship only after being admitted to the university.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Opens: 15 October 2025
Application Deadline: 31 January 2026

Selection Criteria
Candidates are selected based on:
Academic excellence
Research potential and motivation
Quality of recommendation letters
Alignment with ANSO’s mission to promote sustainable scientific development

About The Program

The Summer Research Fellowship Programme 2026 is offered jointly by the Indian Academy of Sciences, Bengaluru, the Indian National Science Academy, New Delhi, the National Academy of Sciences, India, Prayagraj. The fellowship is a prestigious opportunity designed to support outstanding students and teachers to pursue research in science, engineering, and medicine. Selected students will receive a fellowship opportunity.

Source: ias.ac.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:
For Students:

be studying in one of the following courses:
BS/BSc (2nd year only)
BSc (Research) (3rd year only)
BStat/BMath (2nd year only)
BPharm (3rd and 4th years only)
BE/BTech/BCA (2nd and 3rd years only)
BArch (2nd, 3rd and 4th years only)
MS/MSc/MVSc/MPharm (1st year only)
ME/MTech/MCA/MArch (1st year only)
MStat/MMath (1st year only)
5-year integrated MS/MSc/MTech/MBBS/BVSc (2nd, 3rd, 4th years only)
PharmD (3rd, 4th years only)
Dual degree BTech+MTech (2nd, 3rd, 4th  years only)
Dual degree BE+MSc (2nd, 3rd, 4th years only)
Dual degree BS+MS (2nd, 3rd, 4th years only)
Integrated PhD (1st, 2nd years only)
MSc Tech (1st, 2nd years only)
have a minimum of 65% average marks in core subjects (excluding languages) from Class X up to post-graduation
be teaching in a college/university to apply as teachers

(Note: PhD Students are not eligible for this fellowship.)

Benefits

The selected candidates will receive an opportunity to pursue a fellowship programme under the guidance of dedicated and experienced mentors.

Documents
Mark sheets from Class 10 to till the last examination (JPEG format, max 700 KB each)
Contact details of the present teacher (for student applicant)
Project proposal(250 words)
Write-up about research interests (150-250 words)
Publications, if any (for teacher applicants)
Authorisation letter from Principal/HoD (for selected teacher applicants, to be sent by post)

Note: 

Both student and teacher applicants must upload scanned versions of mark sheets from class X till the last examination in JPEG Format (the size of each mark sheet should not exceed 700 KB).
Student applicants should provide the email ID of one of their teachers familiar with their work. The Academy will approach them for a recommendation letter in the prescribed format.
 
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:

Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Scroll down to ‘Click here to Register’.
Step 4: Select your ‘Category option’.
Step 5: Click on the tick box for the ‘Broad area of interest’.
Step 6: Select the sub-area of interest.
Step 7: Fill in the required information, upload the required documents, and click on the ‘Submit’ button.

Note: Applications should be submitted by logging in to either one of these websites: www.ias.ac.in; www.insaindia.res.in; and www.nasi.org.in
 

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 31 January 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on the sole discretion of the academies.

Terms and Conditions
All decisions by the academies are final.
Only one application per candidate will be considered. If more than one application is submitted, none of the applications will be considered.

About The Program

The Rajarshri Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Fee Reimbursement Scholarship Scheme, Maharashtra 2025-26, has been launched by the Maharashtra State Government to assist economically weaker section (EWS) students in pursuing higher education. The primary objective of this scheme is to enable EBC students to enrol in professional courses like MBBS, BDS, BAMS, BHMS, BPTh, BOT, B.Sc Nursing, BUMS, BP&O, and BASLP. The scheme provides up to 50% reimbursement of tuition fees and development charges to students.

Source: mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:- 

be a domiciled resident of Maharashtra
belong to the Economically Backwards Classes (EBC) category
have applied for a degree course in one of the following disciplines at a government-aided, corporation, or private unaided college:
Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS)
Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS)
Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS)
Bachelor of Homoeopathic Medicine and Surgery (BHMS)   
Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPTh)
B.Sc. Nursing
Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery (BUMS)
Bachelor's in Prosthetics and Orthotics (BPO)
Bachelor's in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (BASLP)
Dual-Degree programs (if applicable)
have secured admission under the general/EBC category
have an annual family income of not more than ₹8,00,000
Benefits

The selected students will receive a 50% fee reimbursement of tuition fees and development fees.

Documents
Domicile certificate
HSC and SSC marksheet (for new applicants)
Parents Income certificate

About The Program

Grant of Additional Scholarship to ST Students for Higher Studies, Andaman and Nicobar 2025-26 is an initiative of the Directorate Tribal Welfare, Andaman and Nicobar Administration, to ST category students studying in graduation and above level. The objective of the scheme is to provide additional financial assistance to the students pursuing higher education after senior secondary i.e. Class 12. The selected candidates will receive ₹1,000 per month.

Source:

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible for this scholarship, an applicant must:- 

be a domicile of Andaman and Nicobar Island
belong to the Schedule Tribe (ST) category
be full-time students pursuing higher studies in any recognised institution by the government, approved by AICTE/UGC, or equivalent bodies, anywhere in the country, such as
All courses leading to a graduate degree, e.g. B.A/B.Sc./B.Com. etc. 
All postgraduate courses, e.g. M.A/M.Sc./M.Com/M.Ed/M. Pharma., etc. 
C.A/I.C.W.A./C.S./I.C.F.A. etc. 
M.Phil, Ph.D., and Postdoctoral programs (D.Litt., D.Sc. etc.) 
Degree and postgraduate level courses including M.Phil, Ph.D, and postdoctoral research in Medicine (Allopathy, Indian and other recognised systems of medicines), Engineering, Technology, Planning, Architecture, Design, Fashion Technology, Agriculture, Veterinary & Allied Sciences, Management, Business Finance/Administration, Computer Science/Applications, etc. 
Postgraduate diploma courses in various branches of Management and Medicine, Commercial Pilot License (including helicopter pilot and multiengine rating) course, etc. 
Graduate/Postgraduate courses leading to degree, diploma, or certificate in areas like Pharmacy (B. Pharm), Nursing (B. Nursing), LLB, BFS, other paramedical branches like Rehabilitation Diagnostics, etc., Mass Communication, Hotel Management & Catering, Travel/Tourism/Hospitality Management, Interior Decoration, Nutrition & Dietetics, Commercial Art, Financial Services(e.g. Banking, Insurance, Taxation, etc.) for which minimum educational qualification is senior secondary (Class 12) 
All post-matriculation level non-degree courses for which educational qualification is a vocational stream, ITI course, 3-year diploma course in polytechnic, etc.  

Note:-  Students pursuing postgraduate courses in medicine shall be eligible for this scholarship provided they are not allowed to practice during the period of their course. 

The following applicants are ineligible to apply for the scholarship:-

Students who, after completing one stage of education, choose to study at the same stage but in a different subject. For example B.Sc after B.A. or B.Com after B.A. or, M.A. in a subject after pursuing an M.A. or its equivalent in another subject, etc.
Students who, after having completed their educational career in one professional course and applying for another professional course, eg. LLB after B.T/B.Ed etc.
Benefits

The selected scholars will receive a scholarship of ₹1,000 per month.

Documents
Domicile certificate
Caste certificate
Academic marksheet/degree/certificates
Front page of savings bank account passbook
How can you apply?

Eligible students can apply by following the steps below:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Students' option on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 4: Now, click on the 'Login' button under the OTR option as it is required to apply for a scholarship on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Step 5: Click on the 'New user? Register yourself' on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 6: Read the details carefully, tick the checkbox, and click on the 'Next' button.
Step 7: Enter a functional mobile number, captcha code, and OTP, then submit to complete the registration process.
Step 8: Fill in the required details and click on 'Save & Register' to complete the 'One-time registration (OTR)' process. (Note:- This is a temporary registration number, students need to download the OTR mobile application for face authentication to complete the OTR process.)
Step 9: After successful registration, navigate to the 'Apply for Scholarship' option and click on the 'Login' button.
Step 10: Select the scholarship, fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.

Note:- All the applicants are advised to fill in the information correctly on the application form.  Once the application is submitted, no changes can be made. Additionally, submitting multiple applications will result in disqualification.

Apply Now

Selection Criteria

The candidates will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria.

Scholarship Guidelines
Terms and Conditions
The additional scholarship will be payable for ten months in an academic session.
The additional scholarship money will be directly released to the bank account of the beneficiaries by the Directorate of Tribal Welfare.
If an ST beneficiary pursuing a course fails in the yearly examination for the first time, the scholarship may be renewed. For second and subsequent failures in any class, the student shall bear their expenses until they secure promotion to the next higher class. 
Students who fail to clear Bachelor courses in Arts/Science/Commerce for which they had been awarded this additional scholarship, and join any other recognized Professional or Technical certificate/Diploma/ Degree courses will be awarded an additional scholarship if they are otherwise eligible.
Students in Class 11 or 12 of Higher Secondary School courses are not eligible for this scholarship as it does not apply to non-higher/professional courses. However, if the Class 10 examination is treated as equivalent to Matriculation and students pursue recognised professional/technical courses after passing Class 10, they will be considered post-matric students and eligible for the additional scholarship.

About The Program

The Odisha Banishree Scholarship 2026 is offered through the Social Security and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department, Government of Odisha, for students with disabilities. The aim is to help students with special needs engage in their studies and receive vocational training to become self-sufficient and contributing members of society. Selected candidates will receive up to ₹700 per month.

Source: scholarship.odisha.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be a bonafide resident of Odisha
possess a valid Unique Disability Identity (UDID) card
be pursuing studies in the following
Primary school (Classes 1-5)
Middle & high school (Classes 6-12)
Undergraduate (+2 and +3)
Postgraduate
Technical/ vocational/ professional training

Note:-

Neither of the applicant's parents shall be an income taxpayer.
Students enrolled in special schools or distance education recognised by the government are also eligible.
Benefits
Particulars	Scholarship Rate (₹ per month)
Primary School	400
Middle & High school (not covered by the government of India scholarship)	500
Undergraduate (not covered by the government of India scholarship)	600
Postgraduate (not covered by the government of India scholarship)	700
Technical and vocational training (not covered by the government of India scholarship)	700
Students outside the state (not covered by the government of India scholarship)	700
For visually challenged candidates, Reader's Allowance will be ₹200 per month for primary classes, ₹300 for middle and high school, and ₹400 for other studies, provided the head of the institution certifies continuous employment of a reader, and the assistance is not from the Govt. of India.
For orthopaedically handicapped students with 75% or more disabilities needing special transport arrangements, a monthly allowance of ₹200 may be granted if not covered by the integrated education scheme.
Documents

Academic transcripts
UDID card issued by the CDMO
Bank details

The IIIT Hyderabad Amgen Scholars Program 2026 is an opportunity offered by Amgen India and Harvard University to undergraduate students from Asian countries. The selected candidates shall receive a stipend of USD 2,000 (₹1,77,403 approx.) and other benefits.

Source: amgen.com

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be enrolled as an undergraduate (or equivalent) at a university in an eligible Asian country or area
return to home institution for at least one semester to continue undergraduate studies after completing the program
have completed at least one year of undergraduate study by June 7, 2026.
Be pursuing an undergraduate program may be in any discipline, but must include mathematics as a subject.
be available to attend full-time from June 7 to August 6, 2026, with no other engagements during this period.
have good working knowledge of English, demonstrated by either:
All undergraduate courses taught in English, or
Acceptable scores in one of the following tests:
TOEFL iBT: 72 or above
IELTS: 5.5 overall band score or above
Cambridge English FCE
TOEIC: 1095 or above
TOEIC L&R: 785 or above
TOEIC S&W: 310 or above
Benefits

The selected candidates shall receive:

a stipend of USD 2,000 (₹1,77,403 approx.), disbursed in two installments: half at the commencement of the program and the remaining half upon conclusion.
economy class airfare for travel from the home institution city to Hyderabad, from Hyderabad to Tokyo, and for the return journey from Tokyo to the home institution city.
on-campus accommodation for the duration of the program
meals or a food allowance

About The Program

The IIM Rohtak Doctoral Programme in Management 2026-30 is an opportunity offered by the Indian Institute of Management, Rohtak, for eligible students pursuing a full-time, residential Ph.D. under available disciplines. Selected candidates will receive up to ₹50,000 per month with other benefits.

Source: iimrohtak.ac.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 17-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must meet one of the following qualifications:-

A Post-Graduate degree or equivalent in any discipline with at least 55% marks or an equivalent CGPA.
A Bachelor’s degree with at least 55% marks, along with qualifications in CA, ICWA, or CS.

An MBBS degree with at least 55% marks or an equivalent CGPA, plus 12 months of experience as of May 15, 2026.

Hostel and medical facilities

In addition to this, a merit scholarship (subject to satisfactory performance) of ₹10,000 per month in the second year and ₹15,000 per month in the third and fourth years is given to the deserving participants. About The Program

Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students, Tripura 2025-26 is an initiative of the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India for OBC category students studying at the post-matriculation level. The scholarship aims to aid students financially so they can pursue higher education. The selected students will receive a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹750 and other benefits for the complete duration of the course.

Source: scholarships.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be an Indian citizen
be a domicile of Tripura
belong to the Other Backward Class (OBC) community (as notified by the Central or State Government/UT administration)
be studying post-matriculation or post-secondary courses in a recognised institution/school/university
have an annual parent(s)/guardian(s) income of not more than ₹1,50,000, including their own income (if employed)

The following students are ineligible for the scholarship scheme:-

The scholarships are open to Indian nationals belonging to Other Backward Classes (OBCs) as notified by the Central Government, State Government, or UT Administration.
These scholarships are awarded for the study of all recognised post-matriculation or post-secondary courses pursued in recognised institutions.
Applicants must belong to the OBC category as specified for the state/UT where they are permanently settled.
Applicants must have passed Matriculation, Higher Secondary, or any higher examination from a recognised University or Board of Secondary Education.
Candidates studying in the same stage of education in different subjects (e.g., I.Sc. after I.A., B.Com. after B.A., or M.A. in one subject after M.A. in another) are not eligible.
Students who have completed their education in one professional line (e.g., LLB after B.T./B.Ed.) are not eligible.
Students enrolled in Class 11 of Higher Secondary School or Class 12 of Multipurpose High School are not eligible as it's considered a continuous school course. However, students who pass the tenth class of these courses (considered equivalent to Matriculation) and then join other courses are eligible as post-matric students.
Students pursuing postgraduate medical courses are only eligible if they are not allowed to practice during their studies.
Students who join recognized professional or technical certificate/diploma/degree courses after failing or passing undergraduate/postgraduate examinations in Arts/Science/Commerce are eligible for scholarships if they meet other requirements. Subsequent failures are not condoned except for courses in Group 'A', and no further course changes are allowed after receiving the scholarship.
Students pursuing courses through correspondence (including distance and continuing education) are eligible for reimbursement of non-refundable fees. Additionally, they are eligible for an annual allowance of ₹900 for essential/prescribed books.
Benefits

The selected candidates will receive the benefits in the following manner:

i) Maintenance Allowance

Course	Hostellers (in ₹ per month)	Day Scholars (in ₹ per month)


Group A

Degree and postgraduate level courses including M.Phil, PhD, Post-Doctoral research in Medicine (Allopathic, Engineering, Technology, Planning, Architecture, Design, Fashion Technology, Agriculture, Veterinary and Allied Sciences, Management, Business Finance/Administration, Computer Science/Applications)
Commercial Pilot License courses from GOI institutes (including helicopter pilot and multiengine rating)(Maximum 20 slots per annum): In addition to maintenance allowance, an amount of ₹5,000 per flying hour for a maximum of 200 hours will be paid by the state govt with the approval of the govt of India
Postgraduate diploma courses in various branches of management and medicine
CA/ICWA/CS/ICFA etc
MPhil, PhD and Post-Doctoral programmes (DLitt, DSc, etc)
LLM
	750	350


Group B

Graduate/postgraduate courses leading to a degree, diploma, or certificate in areas like Pharmacy (B.Pharma), Nursing (B Nursing), LLB, BFS, other paramedical branches like rehabilitation diagnostics etc., Mass Communication, Hotel Management and Catering, Travel/Tourism/Hospitality Management, Interior Decoration, Nutrition and Dietetics, Commercial Art, Financial Services (e.g. Banking, Insurance, Taxation etc.) for which entrance qualification is minimum Sr Secondary (10+2)
Postgraduate courses not covered under Group A e.g. MA/M.Sc/M.Com/M.Ed./M.Pharma etc
	510	335

All the other courses leading to a graduate degree not covered under Group A and B e.g. BA/B.Sc./B.Com etc.

	400	210

All post-matriculation level non-degree courses for which entrance qualification is high school (Class X), e.g. senior secondary certificate (Class XI and XII); both general and vocational stream, ITI courses, 3-year diploma courses in Polytechnics, etc.

	260	160

Note: Scholars who are entitled to free boarding and/or lodging will be paid maintenance charges at 1/3rd of the hostellers' rate

 

ii) Reader charges for blind students (Blind scholars)

Group	Reader allowance (in ₹ per month)
Group A and B	175

iii) Fees: Scholars will be reimbursed for the compulsory fees (e.g. enrolment/registration, tuition, games, union, library, magazine, medical exam), excluding refundable deposits like caution money and security deposit.

iv) Study tours: A maximum of ₹900 per annum will be paid to scholars studying professional and technical courses, provided that the head of the institution certifies that the study tour is essential for completion of the course.

v) Thesis typing/printing charges: A maximum of ₹1,000 will be paid to scholars on the recommendation of the head of the institution

Note:- Students who pursue their studies through correspondence courses (i.e. distance and continuing education) will be eligible for reimbursement of a non-refundable fee. Besides reimbursement, such students will also be eligible for an annual allowance of ₹900 for essential/prescribed books, as applicable.

How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Students' option on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 4: Now, click on the 'Login' button under the OTR option as it is required to apply for a scholarship on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Step 5: Click on the 'New user? Register yourself' on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 6: Read the details carefully, tick the checkbox, and click on the 'Next' button.
Step 7: Enter a functional mobile number, captcha code, and OTP, then submit to complete the registration process.
Step 8: Fill in the required details and click on 'Save & Register' to complete the 'One-time registration (OTR)' process. (Note:- This is a temporary registration number, students need to download the OTR mobile application for face authentication to complete the OTR process.)
Step 9: After successful registration, navigate to the 'Apply for Scholarship' option and click on the 'Login' button.
Step 10: Select the scholarship, fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.

Note:- All the applicants are advised to fill in the information correctly on the application form. Once the application is submitted, no changes can be made. Additionally, submitting multiple applications will result in disqualification.

The Kyoto University Amgen Scholars Program 2026 is an opportunity offered by the Amgen Foundation and Harvard University to students pursuing undergraduate courses in Asia. This scholarship aims to support them financially while they study as vacation scholars at Kyoto University. The selected candidates shall receive a scholarship of 180,000 yen (₹1,04,066 approx.) and other benefits.

Source: amgen.com
 

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

Be enrolled as an undergraduate student (or equivalent) at a university located in an eligible country or area within Asia.​
Plan to return to their home institution and continue undergraduate studies for at least one semester following completion of the program.​
Have completed a minimum of one year of undergraduate study by June 7, 2026.​
Be pursuing an undergraduate degree in any discipline, provided mathematics is one of the subjects studied.​
Be available to participate full-time from June 7 to August 6, 2026, without any conflicting engagements during this period.​
Demonstrate proficiency in English by meeting one of the following criteria:
All undergraduate courses have been conducted in English, or, attainment of acceptable scores in a recognized English proficiency test:
TOEFL iBT: 72 or above
IELTS: Overall band score of 5.5 or above
Cambridge English FCE
TOEIC: 1095 or above
TOEIC L&R: 785 or above
TOEIC S&W: 310 or above
Benefits

The selected candidates shall receive:

a scholarship of 180,000 yen (₹1,04,066 approx.) disbursed in two installments of 90,000 yen each, one at the program's start and the other in mid-July
economy class airline ticket arranged by Kyoto University, covering flights between the nearest airport of the student’s university and Osaka (round trip)
accommodation in Kyoto
Documents
Statement of Purpose
Two recommendation letters
Official academic transcripts
English test scores
How can you apply?

Eligible candidates can apply by following these steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Scroll down to the ‘Applications’ section. There under the sub head of ‘Online Application’ click on the ‘Apply to the program’ link. 
Step 4: Click on the blue colour ‘Sign Up’ button. Fill in all the mentioned information and click on ‘Create an Account’ to proceed with the application.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 01 February 2026 at 3PM JST

Selection Criteria

The scholars are selected based on their eligibility and academic merit.

Terms and Conditions
Students are responsible for arranging their own private health insurance and must enroll in Kyoto University’s “Co-op Liability Insurance” upon arrival, as neither the Amgen Foundation nor Kyoto University provides health insurance.
Their attendance is mandatory at the Amgen Scholars Asia Symposium, held either in Tokyo or Kyoto during the program period.

About The Program

The KWI International Fellowship Programme 2025 is offered by the Kulturwissenschaftliches Institut Essen (Institute for Advanced Study in the Humanities). The fellowship is a prestigious opportunity designed to support outstanding international researchers and scholars conducting advanced research in cultural studies, humanities, and social sciences. Selected candidates will receive a monthly allowance of €3,000.

Source: kulturwissenschaften.de

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 28-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:

be an international researcher or scholar 
holds a PhD degree
have up to six years of post-doctoral experience
have a research project that aligns with KWI's research focus areas in cultural studies, humanities, or social sciences
be proficient in English 

Note: 

Post-doctoral experience is counted from the date of PhD completion (PhDs completed in 2020 or later are eligible for this application).
The programme is open to individual researchers only; applications from groups or teams will not be considered.
Early-career and senior researchers from all countries are encouraged to apply.
Benefits

The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of €3,000.

Documents
Curriculum Vitae 
Doctoral degree certificate
Research proposal (maximum 3,000 words)
List of publications
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:

Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below and read all details.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to ‘Register'. (Note: If already registered, log in using the registered email ID)
Step 3: Prepare your documents and combine them into one single PDF file (max. 20 MB).
Step 4: Send the documents to ‘international.fellowship@uni-due.de’ under the subject line of ‘KWI INTERNATIONAL FELLOWSHIPS’.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 28 February 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on eligibility criteria.

Terms and Conditions
The fellowship should be dedicated to research linked to the programmatic agenda of KWI.
Fellows are not expected to teach, but are always welcome to participate in lecture series and seminars at surrounding universities.

About The Program

Reuters-Oxford Journalism Fellowship Programme 2026-27 is an opportunity offered by the Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford to mid-career journalists. This fellowship provides journalists to explore journalism in further depth. The selected candidates shall receive monthly stipend of £2,000 and other benefits.

Source: reutersinstitute.politics.ox.ac.uk

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 13-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

have a minimum of five years’ journalistic experience, or in exceptional cases be able to demonstrate an equivalent level of journalistic expertise.
not be working in public affairs, public relations, or corporate communications at the time of application.
be working as a journalist (freelance work is acceptable and not a barrier to application).
be a journalist from any region of the world, including Europe, Africa, the Asia-Pacific region, North America, or Latin America.
intend to return to journalistic work after completing the fellowship.
be able to understand and participate in discussions conducted in English.  
Benefits

The selected candidates shall receive:

Monthly stipend of £2,000 ( ₹2,43,777 approx.)
Travel expenses (To and from UK)
Visa expenses
Documents
Project proposal (one-page, PDF format)
Motivation letter (one-page)
Curriculum Vitae (two-page, PDF format)
Two letters of reference (one-page each, PDF format)
Summary of proposed project idea (150 words)

(Note: The maximum permissible size limit for PDFs is 25 MB.)

About The Program

The PM YASASVI Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for OBC, EBC, and DNT Students, Haryana 2025-26 is an opportunity offered by the Directorate of Higher Education, Haryana, to students pursuing post-matric level education. The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to the OBC, EBC and DNT students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stages to enable them to complete their education. The selected candidates will receive academic allowances and other benefits.

Source: scholarships.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 28-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be a domicile of Haryana
belong to the OBC, EBC or DNT category
be studying at a post-metric or post-secondary level of education at a government-recognised institution
have an annual family income not exceeding ₹2,50,000
Benefits

The selected candidates will receive the following benefits - 

Category of Courses	Academic allowance (₹per annum)	Tuition Fees (₹per annum) 	Total (₹)
Group 1 - Degree and Postgraduate level professional courses	10,000	10,000	20,000
Group 2 - Other professional courses leading to Degree, Diploma, and Certificate	8,000	5,000	13,000
Group 3 - Graduate and Postgraduate courses that aren't covered in Groups 1 and 2 	6,000	2,000	8,000
Group 4 - All post-matriculation (Post Class 10 level) non-degree courses	5,000	-	5,000

 

Note:-

Students studying through correspondence/online courses shall not be eligible for the academic allowance. However, tuition fees will be paid to them.
Students pursuing C A. /I.C.W.A. /C.S. /I.C.F.A. shall be treated as day scholars for academic allowance.
Documents
Aadhaar card
Applicant’s photograph
Applicant's signature
Income certificate

Parivar Pehchaan Patra

How can you apply?

Eligible students can apply by following the steps below:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Students' option on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 4: Now, click on the 'Login' button under the OTR option as it is required to apply for a scholarship on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Step 5: Click on the 'New user? Register yourself' on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 6: Read the details carefully, tick the checkbox, and click on the 'Next' button.
Step 7: Enter a functional mobile number, captcha code, and OTP, then submit to complete the registration process.
Step 8: Fill in the required details and click on 'Save & Register' to complete the 'One-time registration (OTR)' process. (Note:- This is a temporary registration number, students need to download the OTR mobile application for face authentication to complete the OTR process.)
Step 9: After successful registration, navigate to the 'Apply for Scholarship' option and click on the 'Login' button.
Step 10: Select the scholarship, fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.

Note:- All the applicants are advised to fill in the information correctly on the application form.  Once the application is submitted, no changes can be made. Additionally, submitting multiple applications will result in disqualification.

Terms and Conditions
30% of the funds allocated are reserved for girl students and 5% for students with disabilities

All children of the same parents/guardian can avail themselves of the benefits of this scholarship.

The scholarship will not be paid for the period of internship/horsemanship in the M.B.B.S. course or for practical training in other courses if the student receives some remuneration during the internship period or some allowance/stipend during the practical training in other courses.

About The Program

Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Hostel Maintenance Allowance Scheme, Maharashtra 2024-25 is an initiative of the Directorate of Medical Education Research, Government of Maharashtra, to provide financial assistance to students pursuing specified medical courses in government-aided/corporation/private unaided colleges. Selected students will receive a hostel maintenance allowance of up to ₹30,000 per annum.

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:- 

be a permanent resident of Maharashtra

Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS)

Bachelor of Homoeopathic Medicine and Surgery (BHMS)

Dual-Degree programs
have taken admission in hostels located in major cities of Maharashtra like Mumbai, Pune, Aurangabad, Nagpur, or other locations
have an annual family income of less than ₹8,00,000

Benefits

Category	Students with an annual family income of less than ₹1,00,000 (for a duration of 10 months) 	Students whose parents are registered labourers/Alpabhudharak shetkari (marginal landholders) (for a duration of 10 months)

About The Program

The Ute and György Széll International PhD Scholarship 2026-27 is offered by the Hans Böckler Foundation (Hans-Böckler-Stiftung) (HBS). The scholarship is a prestigious financial award designed to support outstanding international PhD students pursuing research in the social sciences and humanities. Selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of €1450 and other benefits.

Source: boeckler.de

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 28-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be enrolled as a PhD student in Social Sciences or Humanities
have a research plan describing your doctoral project 
secure an invitation from a host PhD supervisor in Germany
have a research paper outlining the topic, questions, methodology, and goals of your research in Germany
be available for a six-month research stay at a German university, research institute, foundation, or archive

Note:- There is no age limit for applicants.

Benefits

The selected students will receive:

A monthly stipend of €1,450 (approx.₹1,52,355.85) for living expenses
An annual allowance of €1,300 (approx.₹1,36,594.90) for travel expenses
A six-month research stay at a German university or research institution

A research paper

About The Program

The UTokyo Amgen Scholars Program 2026 is an opportunity offered by Amgen Foundation and Harvard University to students pursuing undergraduate courses in Asian Institutions. The selected candidates shall receive a stipend of 280,000 JPY(₹1,62,169 approx.).

Source: amgen.com

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be enrolled as an undergraduate (or equivalent) at a university in an eligible Asian country or area
return to home institution for at least one semester to continue undergraduate studies after completing the program
have completed at least one year of undergraduate study by June 7, 2026.
Be pursuing an undergraduate program may be in any discipline, but must include mathematics as a subject.
be available to attend full-time from June 7 to August 6, 2026, with no other engagements during this period.
have good working knowledge of English, demonstrated by either:
All undergraduate courses taught in English, or

TOEFL iBT: 72 or above

A stipend of 280,000 JPY(₹1,62,169 approx.), which may be used freely for food, leisure, and local travel expenses, including short-distance transportation related to the program and travel between home, airports, and residence in Tokyo.
Off-campus accommodation near the Hongo/Yayoi Campus.
Economy-class airfare to Tokyo or round-trip train tickets for domestic students will be covered.
(Note:- Travel plans and the most economical route will be coordinated between participants and the program office, though reimbursement is required if personal schedule changes alter initial travel arrangements.)

About The Program

The Maastricht University (UM) NL-High Potential Scholarship 2025  is an opportunity provided by the Maastricht University and the Dutch Ministry of Education, Culture and Science to postgraduate applicants at the university. This scholarship supports the education of highly talented students. The selected candidates shall receive tuition fees, living expenses of up to €30,625 and other benefits.  

Source: maastrichtuniversity.nl  

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

hold nationality in a country outside the EU/EEA, Switzerland, or Surinam
meet the requirements for obtaining an entry visa and residence permit for the Netherlands
not possess dual nationality from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland
have applied for admission to the full-time master's programme at Maastricht University for the academic year 2026-2027
meet the specific admission requirements of the UM master’s programme applied for
not have previously participated in a degree-seeking higher education programme in the Netherlands (Note:- students who have completed exchange programmes in the Netherlands are eligible to apply.) 
be no older than 35 years of age on 1 September 2026
have achieved excellent academic results in prior education, demonstrated by a latest grade transcript showing a GPA of 7.5 out of 10 or higher
agree to actively serve as a UM NL High Potential and UM ambassador during and after studies by sharing experiences in at least one information session for students and/or other stakeholders
Benefits

The selected scholars shall receive:   

Tuition fees
Living expenses - € 1,225 per month covers the following expenses
Housing: € 600
Groceries and personal hygiene: € 160
Telephone: € 60
Travel: € 100
Books and study materials: € 90
Clothing, hobbies and going out: € 125
Miscellaneous: € 90
Health and liability insurance
Cost of visa application
Pre-academic training expenses
International Student Ambassador Programme (ISAP) cost
Accommodation

About The Program

Centrally Sponsored Pre-Matric Scholarship for ST Students (Class 9 & 10), Andaman and Nicobar 2025-26 is an initiative of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India, to provide financial aid to ST students of Classes 9 and 10. The scholarship aims to improve the participation of ST children in Classes 9 and 10 so that they perform better and have a better chance of progressing to the post-matric stage of education. The selected candidates will receive up to ₹525 per month.

Source: scholarships.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible for this scholarship, an applicant must:-

be an Indian citizen
be a domicile of Andaman and Nicobar
belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) community
be studying in Classes 9 and 10 at a recognised institution
not be receiving any other centrally-funded pre-matric scholarship
have an annual family income of less than ₹2,50,000
Benefits

The selected students will receive the benefits in the following manner:-

Benefit	Day Scholars	Hostellers
Monthly scholarship (in ₹ for 10 months)	225 	525 
Annual books and Ad-hoc grants (in ₹)	750 	1,000 


Additionally, a disability allowance will be provided to Divyangjan students for a year in the following manner:

Day scholars: ₹600 per month
Hostellers: ₹800 per month

Note:-  Leprosy-cured students who also have sickle cell anaemia or thalassemia will be eligible to receive the disability allowance, provided they have the necessary medical certificate.

About The Program

The British Academy International Fellowship 2026 is offered by the British Academy. The fellowship is a prestigious award designed to support outstanding early-career researchers pursuing independent research careers in the humanities and social sciences. Selected candidates will receive a two-year fully-funded fellowship at 80% FEC, including salary, research expenses up to £12,000, and relocation costs up to £8,000 at a UK host institution.

be an international citizen (including India)

21 - for UG/Associate

About The Program

Centrally Sponsored Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students, Andaman and Nicobar 2025-26 is an initiative of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India, for ST students studying in Class 11 and above. The scholarship aims to provide financial assistance to students for continuing post-matriculation or post-secondary level studies. The selected candidates will receive a maintenance allowance of up to ₹1,200 and other benefits.

Source: scholarships.gov.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible for this scholarship, an applicant must:-

be an Indian citizen
be a domicile of Andaman and Nicobar
belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category
be studying at post-matriculation or post-secondary level (i.e. Class 11 to Ph.D. level)
have passed matriculation or higher secondary or any higher examination of a recognised University or Board of Secondary Education 
have an annual family income of less than ₹2,50,000
Benefits

The selected scholars will receive the following benefits:-

Maintenance allowance

Group	Courses	Hosteller (in ₹ per month)	Day Scholars (in ₹ per month)
Group 1	Bachelor's degree, Master's degree, M.Phil/Ph.D. degree   leading to Degree, PG Diploma, in professional courses in various streams 	1,200	550
Group 2	All non-professional recognized courses leading to a Bachelor's degree, Master's degree, M.Phil/Ph.D. degree not covered under Group 1 in Arts, Science and Commerce like BA/B.Sc/B.Com or MA/MSc/M.Com	820	530
Group 3	Vocational stream, ITI courses, three-year diploma courses in Polytechnics, etc. 	570	300
Group 4	All post-matriculation level non-degree courses for which entrance qualification is High School (Class 10), e.g. senior secondary certificate (class 11 and 12)	380	230

Additionally, a disability allowance will be provided to Divyangjan students for a year in the following manner:

Day scholars: ₹600 per month
Hostellers: ₹800 per month

Note:-  Leprosy-cured students who also have sickle cell anaemia or thalassemia will be eligible to receive the disability allowance.

Documents
Domicile certificate
Passport-size photograph
Family's annual income certificate 
Caste certificate duly signed by an authorised Revenue Office
How can you apply?

Eligible students can apply by following the steps below:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Students' option on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 4: Now, click on the 'Login' button under the OTR option as it is required to apply for a scholarship on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Step 5: Click on the 'New user? Register yourself' on the left-hand side of the dashboard and click on it.
Step 6: Read the details carefully, tick the checkbox, and click on the 'Next' button.
Step 7: Enter a functional mobile number, captcha code, and OTP, then submit to complete the registration process.
Step 8: Fill in the required details and click on 'Save & Register' to complete the 'One-time registration (OTR)' process. (Note:- This is a temporary registration number, students need to download the OTR mobile application for face authentication to complete the OTR process.)
Step 9: After successful registration, navigate to the 'Apply for Scholarship' option and click on the 'Login' button.
Step 10: Select the scholarship, fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.

Note:- All the applicants are advised to fill in the information correctly on the application form.  Once the application is submitted, no changes can be made. Additionally, submitting multiple applications will result in disqualification.

The University of Glasgow India Excellence Award 2026 is offered by the University of Glasgow. The scholarship is a prestigious financial award designed to support exceptional students from India pursuing postgraduate taught Master's programmes in any discipline.
