About The Program

NGSF Internship Program 2025 is an internship opportunity offered by the Next Gen Scientists Foundation (NGO registered under the Trust Act of India 1882) for students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate studies who wish to gain research experience in life sciences. The selected interns will receive a stipend of up to ₹6,000 per month and other benefits.

Source: ngsf.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 15-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be interested in undertaking a life science-associated internship for a period of two (minimum of 56 days) or three (minimum of 84 days) months
be a student in:
2nd year of a 3-year undergraduate program, OR
2nd/3rd year of a 4-year undergraduate program, OR
1st year of a 2-year master's program, OR
2nd/3rd/4th year of a 5-year integrated program at an Indian institute
be accepted to work in the lab of a professor/principal investigator (PI) in any Indian Institute outside of their institute of study
ensure the prospective PI mentor has at least three years of experience as faculty and at least three publications in peer-reviewed journals as corresponding author

Note:-

NGSF does not support internships where interns have to pay a fee to gain experience.
Remote internships in Computational Biology are accepted, but will comprise a maximum of 20% of the final selected cohort.
The program does NOT provide internship opportunities directly - it only funds students accepted by PIs.
Students receiving financial assistance from the principal investigator and/or any other agencies (KVPY, INSA, JNCASR, IIT, IISER) are eligible to apply. However, they cannot get additional support from the program.
Benefits

The selected interns will receive a stipend amount of ₹6,000 per month for on-site internships and ₹3,000 for remote internships for a period of up to 3 months upon satisfying the following requirements:

To receive the first month’s stipend, applicants have to get the internship joining letter filled out by the hosting Principal Investigator (PI) and send the scanned version to the Next Gen Scientists Foundation (NGSF).
To receive the second month’s stipend, interns are required to send an article for the NGS blog under the specified guidelines before the first week of the second month of the Internship. Also, the interns are required to fill out the Internship continuation letter.
Interns are required to submit the following:
work progress report for the Internship period within the last week of the second/third month
feedback form at the end of the internship
research work at the end of the internship period in the NGS Interns webinar to receive an internship completion certificate and travel reimbursements

Additionally, on-site interns will receive a reimbursement for their travel expenses, up to the amount it costs to buy a round-trip third-class air-conditioned (III AC) train ticket.

Documents
Scanned university transcripts (in PDF format)
Acceptance letter duly filled by the Principal Investigator (PI) 
Contact details of at least one referee (Note:- A maximum of two references are allowed)
Curriculum Vitae (CV) [Maximum of 2 pages in PDF format)
A research proposal

Note:- The research proposal must be prepared according to the specified standards found on the 'application guidelines' part of the website.

How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Fill in the required information and upload the necessary documents to submit the application.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Start: 15th November 2025
Application Deadline: 15th March 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on merit and fulfilment of the eligibility criteria for an interview via Skype. Final selection will be done thereafter.

Note:- Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Hence, early applications are encouraged.

Important documents
Acceptance LetterJoining LetterContinuation Letter
Terms and Conditions
The internship can span any two to three-month period between May and July, as per the convenience of the student and the principal investigator (PI).
Next Gen Scientists Foundation (NGSF) do not provide internship opportunities directly. The program only funds students accepted by the principal investigator (PI) to work in their labs. A list of the recommended institutes is given on the NGSF website.
Funds from the program are meant to support interns in traditional unpaid internships without fees.
The research proposal carries significant weight in the evaluation process. Emphasis will be given to creativity, understanding of the field, presentation of content, and vision.
All proposals will be checked for plagiarism, and any evidence of plagiarised text will result in immediate disqualification of the applicant.

About The Program

The DAE Doctoral Fellowship Scheme (DDFS) 2026 is offered by the Human Resource Development Division (HRDD), Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC). This prestigious fellowship supports outstanding engineering postgraduates pursuing a Ph.D. under the aegis of Homi Bhabha National Institute (HBNI). Selected fellows will receive ₹80,000 per month along with an annual contingency grant of ₹60,000 for up to 5 years.

Source: barcocesexam.in

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 31-Jan-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be an Indian citizen
hold M.E./M.Tech./M.Sc. (Engg)/Integrated M.Tech degree in Chemical, Computer Science, Electrical, Electronics, Instrumentation, Mechanical, Metallurgy/Material Science, or Civil Engineering with minimum 60% marks (or 6.0 CGPA on 10-point scale)
hold an undergraduate B.E./B.Tech./B.Sc. (Engg) degree in a relevant stream with a minimum 60% marks
be born on or after 1 August 1998 (maximum age 28 years for General category; age relaxation applicable for OBC, SC/ST, and PWD candidates)

Note:- Candidates awaiting final results in academic year 2025-2026 may apply, but must have results up to the 2nd semester of M.Tech (or 9th semester for Integrated M.Tech).

Age relaxation is applicable up to:

3 years in case of Non-creamy Other Backward Class applicants
5 years in case of Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe applicants
10 years of age relaxation for a person with disability
Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

Monthly fellowship of ₹80,000
Annual contingency grant of ₹60,000 for PhD-related expenses
Fellowship for the initial 4 years, extendable to 5 years based on performance
Medical benefits under the Contributory Health Services scheme
Mandatory hostel accommodation during the PhD programme
Documents
Passport-sized photograph (4.5 x 3.5cm)
Scanned signature
Semester 1 & 2 marksheets of the qualifying M.Tech degree
All academic certificates 
Caste certificate (if applicable)
Valid photo ID proof
PWD certificate (if applicable)
No Objection Certificate from employer (if currently employed)
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-

Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Navigate to the ‘DDFS Portal URL’, and click on the ‘Click HERE’ button.
Step 4: Click on the 'Click here for New Registration'. Fill in the required details, and click on the ‘Submit’ button.
Step 5: Verify registered mobile number via OTP and email ID through the verification link sent.
Step 6: Complete the online application form with personal details, academic qualifications, and city preferences for screening examination.
Step 7: Upload required documents.
Step 8: Preview and verify all entered information carefully.
Step 9: Pay the application fee of ₹500 (if applicable) through SBI Payment Gateway and complete the final submission.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 21st January 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected through a two-step process:

Online Screening Examination (CBT): Students must appear for the Computer-based test to be held at multiple centers across India.

Weighted Screening Score: Equal weightage given to marks obtained in M.Tech (average of Semester 1st and 2nd) and DDFS Screening Examination marks.

Selection Interview: Shortlisted candidates will present their M.Tech project (10 minutes) during the interview at BARC Training School, Mumbai—final selection based solely on interview performance, subject to medical fitness.

Important documents
Help Document
Terms and Conditions
Only online applications will be accepted.
Candidates can apply for only ONE DDFS screening discipline.
Application fee of ₹500 + bank charges (exempted for women, SC/ST, transgender, and PWD candidates with 40%+ disability).
No modifications allowed after the final submission of the application.
Travel allowance is not provided for the screening examination.
Two-way AC-III tier train fare provided for selection interview (outstation candidates).
Selected candidates must submit their final marksheet by 31 December 2026.
All academic requirements must be completed before 15 July 2026.
The decision of the competent authority at BARC shall be final and binding.

FAQ's

Question: Who is offering the BARC DAE Doctoral Fellowship Scheme (DDFS) 2026?
Answer: The Human Resource Development Division (HRDD), Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC), under the Department of Atomic Energy, is offering this opportunity.

Question: How much is the fellowship worth?
Answer: The fellowship provides ₹80,000 per month and an annual contingency grant of ₹60,000 for up to 5 years.

Question: What is the last date to apply for the BARC DAE Doctoral Fellowship Scheme (DDFS) 2026?
Answer: The last date to apply for the fellowship is 21 January 2026.

Question: Can I apply if I'm currently in my final year of M.Tech?
Answer: Yes, candidates awaiting final results in academic year 2025-2026 may apply, provided they have results up to the 2nd semester of M.Tech (or 9th semester for Integrated M.Tech).

Question: What is the age limit for this fellowship?
Answer: The maximum age is 28 years for the General category (born on or after 1 August 1998). Age relaxation of 3 years for OBC (non-creamy layer), 5 years for SC/ST, and an additional 10 years for PWD candidates.
 

Question: How many cities are available for the screening examination?
Answer: The online screening examination will be conducted at 52 cities across India.

About The Program

UCL India Excellence Scholarship 2026-27 is an initiative of University College London (UCL) to support the education of bachelor's degree students from India. This scholarship aims to encourage Indian students with exceptional academic record to pursue full-time master's degree studies at UCL. A total of 33 selected candidates will receive a grant of £5,000 for one year (approximately ₹6,27,846).

Source: ucl.ac.uk

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 26-Feb-2026

To be eligible, the applicants must:-

be permanently domiciled in India
hold a bachelor’s degree OR
be enrolled in the final year of a bachelor’s degree in one of the institutions listed in the Top 100 of the Government of India’s National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF )
have secured a GPA in their bachelor’s degree which is equivalent to a UK first-class degree

Note:-

Applicants must have submitted a complete admission application (including references) for a full-time master's degree at UCL in the academic session 2026-27 by 26th February 2026.
This scholarship is not available for students pursuing distance learning programs. Applicants must be willing to attend UCL in person to be eligible for the scholarship.
Benefits

A total of 33 selected candidates will receive a grant of GBP 5,000 (approximately ₹6,27,846) tenable for one year.

Note:- 

The scholarship is valid for one year only, except for two-year full-time programs. In those cases, the scholarship will be divided into two equal payments, one for each year.
The scholarship may be received along with other scholarships, studentships, awards, or bursaries. However, the amount of the UCL scholarship may be adjusted based on the total value of other funding received by the student.
Scholarship recipients must inform the UCL Student Funding Office immediately of any other funding they receive for their UCL Master's degree studies, both before and after receiving the UCL India Excellence Scholarship.
The scholarship will first be used to pay the recipient's tuition fees. Any remaining funds will be paid to the recipient in three equal instalments throughout the academic year to cover living expenses.
Documents
Copy of the current passport
Academic transcripts
A personal statement

Note:- Some programmes require the candidate to provide additional documents for the application. Kindly check the official website's additional programme requirements page to get full details.

How can you apply?

Eligible candidates can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Read the guidelines carefully.
Step 4: Submit a complete admission application to UCL (including the submission of references).

Note:- Applicants will be considered automatically on the basis of their application for admission to their programme of study. There is no separate application form for this scholarship.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 26th February 2026

Selection Criteria
Applicants will be shortlisted based on their bachelor's degree GPA and their university's ranking in the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF).
The final selection will be made based on the information provided in the application for a full-time master's degree at UCL in 2026/27.

About The Program

FIDH ICC Internship 2026 is offered by the International Federation for Human Rights. This internship is a prestigious opportunity designed to support outstanding students pursuing careers in international justice and human rights. Selected candidates will work at FIDH's permanent delegation to the ICC in The Hague, Netherlands, gaining valuable experience in advocacy, research, and engagement with international justice mechanisms.

Source:  fidh.org

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 06-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be pursuing studies in International Law, Criminal Law, Political Science, International Relations, or a related field
have an interest in human rights or international justice with exposure to international organisations or courts
have experience in research and analysis
be fluent in English with a good command of French (Spanish or additional languages are a plus)
have strong organisational and interpersonal skills
Benefits

The selected intern will receive:

monthly stipend in accordance with applicable internal arrangements
daily contribution toward meal expenses
reimbursement of travel expenses
12 days RTT (reduction of working time) over the period
2 days paid holiday per month
Documents
Current CV
Covering letter
Work placement agreement from an educational establishment (compulsory)
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-

Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Prepare your CV and covering letter.
Step 4: Send your application documents to 'recrutement@fidh.org'  with the subject line ‘INTERNSHIPBJI’.

Note:

Interviews will be held as applications are received, so early application is encouraged.
Ensure your work placement agreement from your educational institution is ready if selected.
Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 6th February 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on eligibility criteria.

Terms and Conditions
The intern will work under the direct supervision of Danya Chaikel, FIDH Representative to the ICC, and Ashley Waye, FIDH Programme Officer to the ICC, both based in The Hague.
FIDH is committed to diversity and equality.
For people with disabilities, please indicate in your cover letter any adaptations that might be useful.
Selected candidates will be screened against the Single Asset Freeze List in compliance with UN, EU, and French financial sanctions regulations.
Only candidates whose applications are selected will be contacted.

About The Program

The UK International Undergraduate Excellence Scholarships is a scholarship offered by the University of Nottingham to recognise outstanding international students currently studying in the UK. It aims to support high-achieving applicants who will commence full-time undergraduate studies at Nottingham. Selected students will receive an award of GBP 2,000 in the first year of their chosen program.

Source: nottingham.ac.uk

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 11-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be an international, self-funded, fee-payer student
have achieved AAA grades or international equivalent
hold an offer to begin a full-time undergraduate (RQF level 4) degree programme for 2026-27 at the University of Nottingham, UK
not be receiving any other funding

Note:- Bachelor programmes in Veterinary Medicine and Surgery, Pharmacy, Medicine or Foundation Year are not included under these scholarships.

Benefits

The selected student will receive an award of GBP 2,000 toward tuition fees for the first year of study at Nottingham.

Documents
A duly completed application form
Admission/offer letter
Academic transcripts
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Apply for an undergraduate degree course at the University of Nottingham via UCAS and receive an offer.
Step 4: Login with valid credentials and apply for the scholarship.

Note:- Applicants must apply for a course first.

Apply Now
Important Dates

Application Deadline: 11 March 2026

Selection Criteria
Applications will be assessed based on academic merit and the quality of responses in the scholarship application. 
The scholarship will be awarded after assessing all applications, with outcomes communicated by the end of April 2026. 
This scholarship cannot be combined with other University of Nottingham scholarships — only the highest-value award will apply if multiple awards are offered. 
Applicants who are already externally sponsored are not eligible to apply. 
Terms and Conditions
Recipients must seek a funding team’s approval before making any changes to their programme of study.
Recipients shall notify the funding team if they receive another scholarship from any external source at a later date.
Recipients shall pay their remaining fee before/ on the due date; otherwise, their scholarship will be cancelled.
The scholarship cannot be combined with any other scholarship offered by the University. However, students might be allowed to combine this scholarship with other scholarships, provided the combined grant shall not exceed 100% of the tuition fee.

About The Program

The Heinrich Böll Foundation Doctoral Scholarship 2026  is an opportunity provided by the Heinrich Böll Foundation to Doctoral candidates. This scholarship supports the higher education of highly talented students. The selected candidates shall receive a scholarship of €1650 per month and other benefits.  

Source: boell.de/en

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 01-Mar-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:-

be doctoral candidates from Germany, including German nationals and students with German educational qualifications, pursuing doctoral studies in Germany or other EU countries (all disciplines) or  
be international doctoral candidates from within or outside the EU, including refugees, seeking doctoral positions in Germany (all disciplines).  
be international doctoral candidates who completed their undergraduate studies in their home country are especially encouraged to apply.   
be doctoral candidates from Germany or abroad applying for the “Research Cluster Socio-Ecological Transformation” (all disciplines) 

(Note:- Priority will be given to applicants from DAC countries who are residing in their home country at the time of application.)

Benefits

The selected scholars shall receive: 

€1650 per month
€ 100 mobility allowance per month
Other individual allowances
 
Documents
Application form
Copy of degree certificate or equivalent
Previous academic transcripts
Enrolment certificate
Written proof of good German language skills
Social commitment reference
2 Expert Reports from university lecturers (Academic Reference)
How can you apply?

Eligible candidates can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:-
Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. 
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Scroll down and click ‘To the application portal’
Step 4: Click ‘Register as applicant’
Step 5: Enter all the mentioned details, tick the check box, and click ‘Register’
Step 6: Login to your account with your email ID and password. Fill in all the details, documents, and submit your application.

About The Program

The WFN Junior Travelling Fellowship 2026 is offered by the World Federation of Neurology. This prestigious fellowship is designed to support young neurologists from countries classified by the World Bank as low- or lower-middle-income, enabling them to attend approved international neurology meetings. Selected candidates will receive a fellowship of up to £1,000 towards travel, registration, and conference expenses.

Source: wfneurology.org

Eligibility
Deadline Date: 20-Feb-2026

To be eligible, an applicant must:

be a neurologist in training or early in their career
be from a country classified by the World Bank as low- or lower-middle-income
be no older than 45 years of age at the time of application
hold a post not above that of Associate Professor
hold an M.D. degree or equivalent medical degree
have submitted an abstract for the conference

Note: The transfer of a successful grant award to a different congress is not possible. Please do not apply for meetings taking place before April 01, 2026.

Benefits

The selected candidates will receive a fellowship of up to £1,000 (approx. ₹1,21,986.80).

Documents
Curriculum Vitae
Bibliography
Letter of recommendation from the head of the department
Estimate of expenses (maximum £1,000)
Copy of submitted abstract for the conference
Name and dates of the meeting to be attended
How can you apply?

Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:

Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID).
Step 3: Scroll down to the 'APPLY NOW' button.
Step 4: Click on the ‘Sign up’ button.
Step 5: Fill in the required details and click on the ‘Tick box’. Then click on the ‘Register’ button.
Step 6: Check your Email to verify your account. Click on the ‘CONFIRM MAIL’.
Step 7: ‘Login’ using your credentials and proceed with the application.

Apply Now

Application Deadline: 20th February 2026

Selection Criteria

Candidates will be selected based on eligibility criteria.

Terms and Conditions
After the visit, a report and photos are expected for publication in World Neurology.
Applicants must actively participate in the conference (presentation or poster).
Only conferences taking place after April 01, 2026, are eligible.

About The Program

The Sensodyne IDA Shining Star Scholarship Programme 2025–26 is a CSR initiative by Haleon India, the makers of Sensodyne, in collaboration with Buddy4Study (B4S) and the Indian Dental Association (IDA). The programme aims to empower meritorious yet financially constrained first-year students pursuing the Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) course in government or government-aided colleges across India. As part of Haleon India’s commitment to making oral healthcare education more accessible, selected students will receive financial assistance of ₹2,00,000 disbursed as ₹50,000 per year for four years to support their academic and living expenses.

About Haleon

Haleon is a global leader in the consumer healthcare sector, known for its category-leading brands with a clear mission to deliver better everyday health to humanity. Haleon India (formerly GlaxoSmithKline Consumer Healthcare) is a prominent, fast-moving consumer healthcare company with a diverse product portfolio that includes Oral Healt

Read More...
SENSODYNE IDA SHINING STAR SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAMME 2025-26
Eligibility
Deadline:31-Jan-2026
Open to students in their first year of the Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) program
PAN India students are eligible to apply
Students from government colleges or government-aided colleges who have been awarded a government seat in their respective colleges.
Note: Explicit documentation will be required to prove the same.
Student must have scored a minimum of 60% marks in their previous class/semester.
Students whose family income from all sources is not more than INR 8,00,000 p.a. are eligible.
Note: A certificate not older than 2 years will be required.
Students who are currently receiving any other scholarship are not eligible to apply.
Children of employees of Haleon, Give Grants, and Buddy4Study are not eligible

Note: Once selected, the Sensodyne IDA Shining Star Scholars must maintain a minimum 60% score in each semester/year to continue with the scholarship renewal.

Benefits

Scholarship of ₹50,000 per year for 4 years.

Note: The scholarship can be utilised only for academic expenses such as tuition fees, hostel fees, food, internet, mobile, laptop, books, stationery, online learning, etc.

Documents
Recent passport-size photograph of the applicant
Marksheet of the last academic year or semester
Government-issued ID proof (Voter ID, Driving License, or PAN Card)
Proof of current admission (Fee Receipt, Admission Letter, Institution ID Card, or Bonafide Certificate)
Family income proof (Income Certificate issued by local authorities, EWS Certificate, or latest Income Tax Return)
Bank account details of the applicant or their parent/guardian

Note: Starting from the second year, the scholars must submit an academic grade report of the last exam passed and a character certificate from their college to be eligible for the scholarship in subsequent years.

How can you apply?
Click on the ‘Apply Now’ button below.
Log in to Buddy4Study with your registered ID and land on the ‘Application Form Page’.
If not registered, register at Buddy4Study with your Email/Mobile number/Google account.
You will now be redirected to the ‘Sensodyne IDA Shining Star Scholarship Programme 2025-26’ application form page.
Click on the ‘Start Application’ button to begin the application process.
Fill in the required details in the online application form.
Upload relevant documents.
Accept the ‘Terms and Conditions’ and click on ‘Preview’. 
If all the details filled in by the applicant are correctly showing on the preview screen, click on the ‘Submit’ button to complete the application process.

FAQ’s
What is the selection process for the Sensodyne IDA Shining Star Scholarship Programme 2025-26?

The selection of scholars for the ‘Sensodyne IDA Shining Star Scholarship Programme 2025-26’ will be made based on their academic merit and financial background. It involves a multi-stage process, as detailed below:

Initial shortlisting of applicants based on their academic merit (60%) and annual family income (less than ₹ 8,00,000) provided during the application process.
Document verification of the shortlisted candidates.
Evaluation of shortlisted candidates via telephonic interview.
A final list of selected candidates will be prepared.
